combat_history.event: Fujian departed Jiangnan Shipyard for initial sea trials, testing basic ship systems and propulsion
combat_history.significance: Marked transition from construction to operational testing phase

modernization.description: Refining electromagnetic launch system based on initial testing, improving reliability and launch rates
modernization.impact: Critical for achieving operational sortie rates comparable to U.S. carriers

modernization.description: Training pilots for CATOBAR operations, developing J-35 stealth fighter integration, KJ-600 AEW aircraft trials
modernization.impact: Determines actual operational capability and effectiveness of the platform

vulnerabilities.description: Chinese EMALS system unproven in operational conditions; U.S. Ford-class experienced significant EMALS reliability issues
vulnerabilities.context: Launch system failures could severely limit sortie generation during high-tempo operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Extensive testing program ongoing, likely backup systems installed

vulnerabilities.description: PLAN has limited experience with CATOBAR operations and complex carrier air wing management
vulnerabilities.context: Carrier aviation is extremely complex; mistakes in high-threat environment could be catastrophic
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Extended training periods, possible foreign advisor programs

vulnerabilities.description: Steam turbine propulsion limits operational flexibility compared to nuclear carriers
vulnerabilities.context: Requires regular refueling, reducing time on station and complicating logistics in extended deployments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Large fuel capacity, extensive PLAN tanker fleet development

vulnerabilities.description: Current aircraft complement inferior to U.S. carrier air wings; J-15 has performance limitations, J-35 still in development
vulnerabilities.context: Reduced strike capability and defensive effectiveness against peer adversaries
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Accelerated J-35 development, potential Su-33 variants

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Expected to operate as flagship of PLAN carrier strike groups in Western Pacific
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Type 055 destroyers, Type 052D destroyers, Type 054A frigates, nuclear submarines, supply ships
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Currently in extended sea trials and crew training phase; full operational capability expected 2026-2027

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Ford has nuclear propulsion, more advanced EMALS, larger air wing capacity (75+ aircraft), and vastly more experienced crew. Fujian represents China's first attempt at supercarrier operations.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Technology transfer comparison - how China developed indigenous EMALS and what capabilities remain inferior

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Nimitz class has proven nuclear propulsion, steam catapults with known reliability, and decades of operational experience. Fujian is larger but less proven.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Operational experience gap - why carrier aviation takes decades to master

peer_comparisons.key_differences: QE class uses STOBAR with F-35B, smaller air wing, but proven concept. Fujian chose more complex CATOBAR for greater capability at higher risk.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Design philosophy comparison - STOBAR vs CATOBAR trade-offs

peer_comparisons.key_differences: CdG is nuclear-powered but much smaller (42,000 tons), with proven CATOBAR operations. Shows what mature non-U.S. carrier operations look like.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Learning curve analysis - how long it took France to achieve carrier proficiency

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Vikrant is much smaller (45,000 tons) STOBAR carrier, but represents similar indigenous carrier development challenge. India chose simpler, more proven approach.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Asian carrier race - different national approaches to indigenous carrier development

doctrine.role: Power projection beyond the second island chain to establish PLAN as a global navy capable of challenging U.S. carrier strike groups in contested waters. Fujian serves as the cornerstone of China's transition from regional sea denial to global sea control ambitions.
doctrine.employment: Operates as the centerpiece of a carrier strike group (CSG) including Type 055 cruisers, Type 052D destroyers, Type 054A frigates, and Type 093B attack submarines. Primary missions include establishing sea control in the South China Sea, projecting power into the Indian Ocean, and providing mobile air defense for amphibious operations against Taiwan. Command structure integrates with PLAN's Theater Commands, particularly Southern and Eastern Theater Commands for regional contingencies.
doctrine.design_philosophy: Prioritized aircraft capacity and sortie generation rate over defensive systems, accepting reduced close-in weapons compared to Soviet designs. Designers chose EMALS over steam catapults despite technological risk to maximize aircraft launch flexibility and efficiency. The conventional propulsion sacrifice limits sustained high-speed operations compared to nuclear carriers but reduces complexity and cost for China's first supercarrier attempt.
doctrine.threat_context: Designed to operate within range of U.S. submarine threats and advanced anti-ship missiles, requiring robust escort protection. Originally conceived when U.S. maintained clear naval superiority, but now faces evolving threats from hypersonic weapons, underwater drones, and multi-domain operations that challenge traditional carrier survivability assumptions.
doctrine.comparison_frame: Compare on aircraft capacity, sortie generation rate, and catapult capability rather than speed or range—all modern carriers accept 30+ knot capability as sufficient. Focus on air wing composition flexibility, maintenance cycle efficiency, and integration with national command systems. Defensive capabilities matter less than offensive power projection and sustained operations tempo.

curated_sources.title: China Naval Modernization: Implications for U.S. Navy Capabilities
curated_sources.relevance: Ron O'Rourke's definitive analysis of PLAN carrier development and strategic implications for U.S. naval planning.

curated_sources.title: Chinese Naval Shipbuilding: An Ambitious and Uncertain Course
curated_sources.relevance: Andrew Erickson provides the most detailed technical analysis and construction timelines for Type 003 development.

curated_sources.title: Aircraft Carriers: A History of Carrier Aviation and Its Influence on World Events
curated_sources.relevance: Norman Polmar's comprehensive framework for understanding carrier doctrine evolution provides context for China's carrier ambitions.

curated_sources.title: China's Aircraft Carrier Programme: Drivers, Developments, and Implications
curated_sources.relevance: RUSI analysis of strategic drivers behind China's carrier program and implications for Indo-Pacific balance.

curated_sources.title: Type 003 Aircraft Carrier - Seaforces Online
curated_sources.relevance: Comprehensive technical specifications and construction details for comparative analysis.

curated_sources.title: Military and Security Developments Involving the People's Republic of China
curated_sources.relevance: Annual Pentagon assessment provides official U.S. perspective on Type 003 capabilities and threat assessment.

curated_sources.title: The Science of Military Strategy (2020 Edition)
curated_sources.relevance: Official PLA strategic doctrine publication outlining naval power projection concepts underlying carrier employment.
